commands.text: Go down the stairs?
A friendly Pixie wants to join your party!
The soothing light restores your party's HP and MP!
SPEC S8 composability proof: crafting authored as pure data. CHOICE -> hasItem checks -> TAKE_ITEM -> formula success roll -> GIVE_ITEM_ID / consolation text. Zero SCRIPT, zero engine Lua.
A sturdy workbench, cluttered with alembics and etching tools.
Recipe: 1x HP Tonic + 1x Sigil Ink -> Whispered Lessons. Ingredient checks are presence-only (hasItem has no count param), so recipes use two distinct items.
Consume first, then roll: flat 75% success chance.

else.fallback: The attempt fails; the materials are ruined...

elseCommands.text: You are missing: Sigil Ink.
You are missing: HP Tonic.

commands.text: Recipe: 1x Mystic Egg + 1x Sigil Ink -> Golden Egg. Ingredient checks are presence-only (hasItem has no count param), so recipes use two distinct items.
Consume first, then roll: success scales with average party level, 35%..85%.

else.fallback: The attempt fails; the materials are ruined...

elseCommands.text: You are missing: Sigil Ink.
You are missing: Mystic Egg.

commands.text: Recipe: 1x Mug of Ale + 1x Pint of Stout -> Glass of Wine. Ingredient checks are presence-only (hasItem has no count param), so recipes use two distinct items.
Consume first, then roll: a steady hand comes with a full purse: 50% +0.1%/gold, capped 90%.

then.fallback: Success! You crafted Glass of Wine.

else.fallback: The attempt fails; the materials are ruined...

elseCommands.text: You are missing: Pint of Stout.
You are missing: Mug of Ale.

commands.text: You step away from the workbench.
(She fiddles with a loose thread on her apron, waiting for you to speak.)
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: Laura? She’s… she’s amazing. She’s strong, and beautiful, and… and she smells like vanilla and iron. Is that weird? That’s weird. Forget I said that.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: I sell things that keep you alive! It makes me happy. Watching people leave with full bags… it means they might come back. That’s nice.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: Me? I’m just Alicia. I like baking. And… I like listening to the rain. The dungeon is scary, but the rain makes it sound like it’s sleeping.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: I’m going to make this into a necklace. Or maybe a ring? No, a necklace. She doesn’t like rings when she works. Thank you again!
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: The Glittering Teardrop… please be careful. It’s pretty, but the dungeon is mean. Don’t get hurt for a rock, okay?
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: You… you found it! Oh, look at it! It’s perfect. It catches the light just like her eyes when she’s happy. Thank you! Thank you so much!
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: I’m going to make this into a necklace. Or maybe a ring? No, a necklace. She doesn’t like rings when she works. Thank you again!
commands.speaker: Alicia

elseCommands.text: Actually… can I ask a favor? It’s about Laura. I want to give her something special. Something that… shines like she does. There’s a crystal, a ‘Glittering Teardrop’, deep in the dungeon. Would you…?
elseCommands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: The Glittering Teardrop… please be careful. It’s pretty, but the dungeon is mean. Don’t get hurt for a rock, okay?
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: You… you found it! Oh, look at it! It’s perfect. It catches the light just like her eyes when she’s happy. Thank you! Thank you so much!
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: I’m going to make this into a necklace. Or maybe a ring? No, a necklace. She doesn’t like rings when she works. Thank you again!
commands.speaker: Alicia

elseCommands.text: Oh! Are you looking for the supply cart? I heard it! I was listening to the wind near the chasm, and I heard squeaky wheels stop suddenly. Then... goblin noises. Lots of them.
elseCommands.speaker: Alicia

options.label: Goblins? I should tell the guard.

commands.text: Please be careful! If the goblins have the food, they'll be... energetic.
commands.speaker: Alicia

elseCommands.text: Oh! H-hello—! I… I didn’t see you coming. I’m Alicia. Welcome to my shop! Uhm— please look around! And— and if you’re hungry, I can warm something up. Not for sale. Just… because.
elseCommands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: M-me? No, I’m… I’m normal. I just… I don’t like when people look empty. Like they forgot they have a body. Sorry. That sounded weird.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: …Thank you. People usually laugh. Or they say ‘that’s cute’ and then they don’t mean it. Anyway! Please— uhm— browse!
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: (She wipes soot from her forehead, giving you a brief moment of attention.)
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Alicia? She's... soft. Too soft for this place. But she makes good bread. And she doesn't ask stupid questions. I tolerate her.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Fire purifies. Metal obeys. People... people lie. Steel never lies to you. It just is. That's why I like it.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: I'll need time to work on this. Check back later. And... thanks.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Find that signet. Don't come back empty-handed.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: You found it. Hmph. Not bad. The gold is pure... untouched. Give it here.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: I'll need time to work on this. Check back later. And... thanks.
commands.speaker: Laura

elseCommands.text: You're going deep? Listen. Rumor says a rare undead down there wears a golden band. Unmarked, untarnished. I want that signet — new material for my forge, good pay for you. Interested?
elseCommands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Find that signet. Don't come back empty-handed.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: You found it. Hmph. Not bad. The gold is pure... untouched. Give it here.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: I'll need time to work on this. Check back later. And... thanks.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Find that signet. Don't come back empty-handed.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: You found it. Hmph. Not bad. The gold is pure... untouched. Give it here.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: I'll need time to work on this. Check back later. And... thanks.
commands.speaker: Laura

elseCommands.text: Hmm? Oh. You're the new one. Eyes forward, don't drag your feet. The smithy is hot, and I don't have time for tourists.
elseCommands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Steel doesn't shape itself. If you're not buying or repairing, get out of the smoke.
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Let me see that... Hah! Yes. The core is intact. The steel is ancient, folded a thousand times. Give me an hour.
Done. I've added my best work to the shop inventory. Special stock, just for you. Don't dull it on a rat's skull.
Find that scrap metal yet? The forge is waiting.
Good to see you alive. The forge is hot, and the steel is singing today. Need anything?

elseCommands.text: Blades, hammers, axes. If it kills, I sell it. If it breaks, I fix it. If you break, that’s on you.

commands.text: Down there? Don’t fight fair. Don’t fight pretty. Fight to leave. The only winning move is coming back with your hands still yours.
You want to know how to not die? Fine. Listen close.
Swords are balanced. Axes hit hard but miss if you're clumsy. Magic weapons... well, they depend on your mind, not your muscle. Pick what fits your hand.
Heavy plate stops claws, but it slows you down. If you're slow, you get hit more. Sometimes dodging is better than tanking. Your choice.
The blade is done. Check my wares if you have the coin.
Find that scrap metal yet? The forge is waiting.

elseCommands.text: Interesting? Maybe. Legends say a 'Shattered Blade' lies in the Cryptic Vault. Floor 4, maybe 5. It's useless scrap to you, but I could reforge it into something... lethal.

commands.text: So Alicia heard them stop near the chasm? Damn. That means goblins or worse. Thanks for the intel. I'll double the watch.
You did good work. Watch your back out there.

elseCommands.text: Any news on the shipment? Ask Alicia, she usually hears the carts coming.

commands.text: Ready to descend again? Don't make me carry your body out.
You did good work. Watch your back out there.

elseCommands.text: No Guild Pass. Speak to the Guildmaster in town. And don’t try to slip past. I’ve buried faster people than you.

commands.text: Strange? Always. But the supply shipment is late. Very late. It usually comes through the old tunnel, but it's silent.
Rare finds! Highest bidder takes all! …Well. Fixed price for now. But the *spirit* is the same. Spend like you’re trying to forget something.
It means you pay first and ask questions later. Just like love. Just like the dungeon. Now— are you brave, or are you smart?
Efficiency is key. Do not waste my time.
I summon because I don’t trust people to stand where I need them. Creatures are honest. They take payment and they do the job.
Lonely is a luxury. You’ll learn that if you keep surviving.
Heh. You look like you’ve seen a ghost. Want to hear a story? They’re all true. That’s what makes them useless.
Once, a Summoner ran out of MP and begged his own creature to stop draining him. The creature cried. Then it kept eating. The dungeon doesn’t make monsters. It just reveals who’s hungry.
A friendly face in the dark. Care for supplies? It gets expensive the deeper you go. That’s the point.
Advice? Keep one summon slot empty. The dungeon loves to tempt you when you’re at your limit. And never ‘push one more room’ when your MP is low. That’s how you become a story.

elseCommands.text: Oh— a living one. Don’t startle me like that. Here. I sell necessities. Not hope. Hope’s extra.

commands.text: Advice? Keep one summon slot empty. The dungeon loves to tempt you when you’re at your limit. And never ‘push one more room’ when your MP is low. That’s how you become a story.
…Don’t waste steps. Don’t waste summons. Don’t waste yourself.
It doesn’t want you dead. It wants you *convinced*. There’s a difference. Dead things don’t pray. Convinced things do.
A town is just a room with too many mirrors. Everyone here is looking for the angle that makes them forgivable.

elseCommands.text: …You’re late.

commands.text: Daphne. I’m the part of the dungeon that learned to speak politely.
It is. You’re just not used to answers that don’t comfort you.
…Don’t waste steps. Don’t waste summons. Don’t waste yourself.
It doesn’t want you dead. It wants you *convinced*. There’s a difference. Dead things don’t pray. Convinced things do.
A town is just a room with too many mirrors. Everyone here is looking for the angle that makes them forgivable.
For the version of you that doesn’t come back.
…Don’t waste steps. Don’t waste summons. Don’t waste yourself.
It doesn’t want you dead. It wants you *convinced*. There’s a difference. Dead things don’t pray. Convinced things do.
A town is just a room with too many mirrors. Everyone here is looking for the angle that makes them forgivable.
*hic* Whaddya want? Can'tcha see I'm... thinkin'?
Thinkin' 'bout... 'bout how the floor keeps movin'. An' how my mug's always empty. It's a conspiracy, I tell ya... A thirsty, wobbly conspiracy.
Oh! H-hello again. I... I just needed to get out of the shop for a little while. All the dust... it gets in my throat.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: Yes! Everything is... fine. Laura, she... she liked the gift. I think. She's wearing it. Under her apron, so no one else can see. It's... nice. Thank you, again. For helping me.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: (She gives you a genuine, heartfelt smile.)
commands.speaker: Alicia

options.label: It's nice to see you out.

commands.text: Thank you. It's... a little loud. But the tea is good. It's nice to just... sit for a moment. And not worry about what needs restocking.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: Well met, traveler. I'm a collector of sorts. A collector of... numbers.
commands.speaker: The Gambler

commands.text: Excellent! I'll pick a number between 1 and 10. You just have to guess if it's high or low.
You won! Here's a prize.
Come back later.

elseCommands.text: Too bad! Better luck next time.
Come back later.

commands.text: You won! Here's a prize.
Come back later.

elseCommands.text: Too bad! Better luck next time.
Come back later.

commands.text: Come back later.
The air in The Rusty Tankard is thick with the smell of cheap ale and roasted meat. Laughter and hushed whispers compete with the clinking of glasses.
You notice Alicia sitting by herself at a small table, nervously sipping a cup of tea. It's unusual to see her outside her shop.
In a dimly lit corner, you spot a familiar figure shuffling a deck of cards with practiced ease.
An old mercenary sits by the fire, staring into the flames. He looks like he has stories to tell.

elseCommands.text: You find an empty table. It's nice to sit down for a moment, but there's no one interesting nearby right now.

commands.text: Oh! H-hello there! It's good to see you again. Please, look around. And— uh— drink water. People forget.
commands.speaker: Alicia

commands.text: Back again? Blade dull already? Or are you just looking for warmth?
commands.speaker: Laura

commands.text: Guild Pass verified. Keep your MP above the red line. Don’t be a hero in there— heroes become stains.
People come back talking about ‘treasure’. Then they stop sleeping. Then they stop talking. Then we stop calling them by name. Go.
You... you cleared the Sanctum? The air feels lighter today. Maybe it's not a coincidence. You're making history, Summoner.
The Rusted Choir is silent? Good. Those bells were driving the dogs mad. Keep pushing deeper.

elseCommands.text: Still alive. That's progress enough for today.

commands.text: Strange? Always. But the supply shipment is late. Very late. It usually comes through the old tunnel, but it's silent.
The bartender nods at you as he wipes a glass with a rag that's seen better days. 'What can I get you?'
commands.speaker: NPC_Barkeep

commands.text: Rain's coming. My knee aches. Means the slimes on the first floor will be extra sticky.
Some fool said he saw a golden egg on the third floor. Probably just a trick of the light, or a trap.
If you see a statue that looks like it's crying, don't touch it. Unless you like curses.
Alicia's been baking again. Smells like burnt cinnamon. Poor girl tries hard, though.

elseCommands.text: News? The dungeon's deep, the monsters are hungry, and the beer is cold. That's all the news that matters.

commands.text: You want to know how to not die? Fine. Listen close.
Swords are balanced. Axes hit hard but miss if you're clumsy. Magic weapons... well, they depend on your mind, not your muscle. Pick what fits your hand.
Heavy plate stops claws, but it slows you down. If you're slow, you get hit more. Sometimes dodging is better than tanking. Your choice.
